title: StudioDash Revenue Analytics
description: StudioDash revenue analytics for App Store revenue, advertising revenue, provider totals, and historical trends.
summary: StudioDash revenue analytics are designed to help developers understand product income across App Store, advertising, and website monetisation sources.

featureShowcases.heading: Revenue provider breakdown
featureShowcases.summary: The revenue analytics screen brings provider totals, product income, and trend context into one review area.
featureShowcases.body: The screen helps developers understand whether a revenue movement appears in App Store data, advertising data, website monetisation, or a provider that has not returned data. That distinction is important for deciding whether to investigate product performance, provider setup, or reporting coverage.
bullets: Shows revenue sources and provider context where data is available.
Supports selected-period comparison and historical trend review.
Avoids treating pre-release or unavailable provider data as real income.

image.alt: StudioDash revenue analytics screen showing provider totals, revenue breakdowns, and trend panels
image.caption: Revenue analytics are framed around provider clarity and practical follow-up.

sections.heading: Revenue overview
paragraphs: StudioDash is designed to give developers a clearer view of revenue across product and advertising channels. The revenue view brings high-level totals, provider rows, app-level breakdowns, country information, date grouping, and trend context into one area.

Where provider history is available, selected-period summaries can help compare current performance with recent periods. Where provider data is not available, StudioDash uses clear no-data or setup messaging rather than presenting pre-release figures as real revenue.

sections.heading: Covered revenue sources
paragraphs: Revenue coverage depends on configured providers and the data each provider exposes. Public guide uses conservative wording while StudioDash remains in development.
bullets: App Store Connect revenue can include Sales and Trends data where reporting access is configured.
AdMob revenue can include advertising performance from OAuth-backed network reporting where available.
AdSense is represented as website monetisation pre-release coverage until live reporting support is implemented.
Provider rows remain visible when data is unavailable so users know which integration is required.

sections.heading: Trend review
paragraphs: The revenue area is intended to support selected-period comparisons, revenue split review, provider totals, and historical trend analysis.
Future reporting improvements may expand exports, scheduled reports, forecasts, and deeper profitability views. Those areas should be treated as roadmap content until they are delivered.
